channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Immigrants in Chicago and other US cities brace for expected Trump deportation arrests
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — The Rev. Homero Sanchez said he didn't realize the depth of fear in the Chicago immigrant community he serves until someone asked him to handle the sale of their family's home and other finances if they are picked up this week when President-elect Donald Trump takes office.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jingle jangle: Draft lyrics to 'Mr. Tambourine Man' sell for $508K at US auction
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Draft lyrics to Bob Dylan’s song “Mr. Tambourine Man” went for over a half-million dollars as part of a weekend sale of dozens of items related to the iconic American singer-songwriter.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's inauguration eve rally
item.description: President-elect Trump on Sunday rallied with supporters in Washington, D.C., on the eve of his second inauguration, taking a victory lap and teasing an expected barrage of activity on his first day in office. Here are five takeaways from Trump’s rally at Capital One Arena.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, WGN 9

item.title: Can you spot black ice? What to do if you start sliding or skidding
item.description: Black ice forms without creating bubbles, meaning it blends in with the road and becomes hard to detect.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, NewsNation

item.title: Fire-ravaged Southern California braces for gusty winds and heightened wildfire risk
item.description: Southern Californians are bracing for gusty winds and a heightened risk of wildfires less than two weeks after deadly blazes that have killed at least 27 people and ravaged thousands of homes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump plans barrage of Day 1 executive actions on border, energy, federal workforce
item.description: President-elect Trump is planning a barrage of executive actions on energy, the border and immigration enforcement on his first day in office, four sources told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Over 100 out-of-state homeless in Hawaii sent back to mainland families
item.description: Hawaii lawmakers claim programs from other states send homeless to the Islands, especially in the winter.
item.dc:creator: Bryce Moore, ABC 4

item.title: Heavy snow and frigid Arctic blast put 70 million across the US under winter storm warnings
item.description: Tens of millions of residents along the East Coast are bracing for several inches of snow Sunday followed by dangerously cold temperatures that will grip much of the country from the Northern Plains to the tip of Maine.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How TikTok grew from a fun app for teens into a potential national security threat
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — If it feels like TikTok has been around forever, that's probably because it has, at least if you're measuring via internet time.
item.dc:creator: DAVID HAMILTON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Convicted NXIVM cult leader's defense accuses FBI of evidence tampering
item.description: Attorneys for convicted NXIVM sex trafficker Keith Raniere say defense experts have uncovered "provable" evidence of tampering in their quest for a new trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Heavy snow and frigid Arctic blast put 70 million across the US under winter storm warnings
item.description: Tens of millions of residents along the East Coast are bracing for several inches of snow Sunday followed by dangerously cold temperatures that will take hold in much of the country from the Northern Plains to the tip of Maine.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Coco Gauff writes 'RIP TikTok USA' on a TV camera at the Australian Open
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — American tennis star Coco Gauff mourned the loss of TikTok's app back home, writing on a TV camera lens “RIP TikTok USA” and drawing a broken heart right after winning a match at the Australian Open to reach the quarterfinals.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Keke Palmer comedy ‘One of Them Days’ and ‘Mufasa’ in close race for No. 1
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Keke Palmer buddy comedy “One of Them Days” opened in first place on the North American box office charts on a particularly slow Martin Luther King Jr. holiday weekend.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Legacy news on its heels as second Trump era begins, but not abandoning mission
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As Donald Trump prepares to assume the presidency for a second time, he faces a news establishment on its heels — but not flat on its back. Last year's presidential campaign suggested that many traditional outlets are not depended upon, or trusted, nearly as much as they used to be.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: These lip balm ingredients may be making your chapped lips worse, experts say
item.description: It's tempting to reach for lip balm during the dry, winter months, but it may not be helping you at all.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Gaza ceasefire takes hold and the first handover of hostages is underway
item.description: A ceasefire between Israel and Hamas took hold in the Gaza Strip on Sunday, ushering in an initial six-week period of calm and raising hopes for the release of dozens of militant-held hostages and an end to the devastating 15-month war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Most voters in new survey seek toned-down Trump agenda
item.description: Over 60% of Americans polled disagree with Trump's idea to replace thousands of career civil-service workers.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, ABC 4

item.title: TikTok says it will 'go dark' unless it gets clarity from Biden following Supreme Court ruling
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — TikTok said it will have to “go dark” this weekend unless the outgoing Biden administration assures the company it won’t enforce a shutdown of the popular app after the Supreme Court on Friday unanimously upheld the federal law banning the app unless it’s sold by its China-based pa
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump says he will ‘most likely’ will give TikTok 90-day extension
item.description: President-elect Trump told NBC News on Saturday that he is likely to give TikTok a 90-day extension while his incoming administration assesses the situation following the Supreme Court decision to uphold a federal ban.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Stray dog brings sick, freezing puppy to vet's office
item.description: A stray dog was caught on camera carrying her sick puppy to the front door of a veterinarian clinic in Turkey earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas police officer, an Army veteran, killed in line of duty during car chase
item.description: A Texas community is mourning the loss of Navasota Police Sgt. Mark Butler, who died while saving others from a wrong-way driver during a pursuit.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vermont accused in lawsuit of tracking pregnant women considered unsuitable to be mothers
item.description: Vermont’s child welfare agency used baseless allegations to secretly investigate a pregnant woman and win custody of her daughter before the baby's birth, according to a lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida hotel discriminated against Arab American group after Hamas' Oct. 7 attack against Israel, DOJ says
item.description: A Florida hotel owner reached an agreement with the DOJ, which accused the hotel of discrimination after it canceled a planned conference for an Arab American group.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Free upgrades and pardon requests: What it's like to share a name with a president
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — There’s not only one Donald Trump in the United States. Nor is there only one William J. Clinton. Plenty of people share names with famous presidents. Donald Trump, a veteran and resident of Alabama, said because of his name he often is gifted things for free. William J.
item.dc:creator: MIKE PESOLI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Navajo Nation firefighters battle wildfires in Los Angeles
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Firefighters from the Navajo Nation worked tirelessly through a haze of dust to cut away dirt from a narrow road at the side of a mountain struck by a landslide in Southern California, coughing and sneezing amid the backbreaking work.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LOCHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: College students accused of online 'catch a predator' plot appear in court
item.description: Six Assumption University students were in court to face charges in a connection made between a Holyoke woman and a man on Tinder, which resulted in a TikTok plot to confront a falsely accused sexual predator.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Shook, NewsNation

item.title: Kevin O'Leary says he's offering $20B cash for TikTok
item.description: Investor Kevin O’Leary, widely known as a star from “Shark Tank,” said he offered TikTok’s owners $20 billion in cash to buy the platform during a Friday appearance on Fox News’s “America’s Newsroom.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Biden admin drops its 'zero tolerance' policy targeting gun dealer licenses over paperwork errors
item.description: The Biden administration has reversed a policy revoking the federal firearms license of gun shops for simple paperwork errors.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California is years behind in implementing a law to make homes more fire resistant
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — Reeling from destructive wildfires, including the deadliest in California history, state lawmakers in 2020 passed new requirements for clearing combustible materials like dead plants and wooden furniture within 5 feet (1.5 meters) of homes in risky areas.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fires scorched campuses across Los Angeles. Many schools are seeking places to hold classes
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Days after losing her home in the same fire that destroyed her Los Angeles elementary school, third-grader Gabriela Chevez-Muñoz resumed classes this week at another campus temporarily hosting children from her school.
item.dc:creator: KRYSTA FAURIA, JOCELYN GECKER and CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Woman mistakenly declared dead after funeral home's typo
item.description: A Maryland woman's life was turned upside down after a simple typo led to her being declared legally dead.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judges hand down what could be the last punishments for the Jan. 6 riot before pardons from Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal judge who oversaw Donald Trump's 2020 election interference case denounced efforts to rewrite the history of the U.S. Capitol attack Friday as she handed down what she acknowledged may be one of the final punishments for the Jan.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wisdom of a California grandmother, 90, goes viral amid Eaton Fire tragedy
item.description: A nearly 90-year-old California woman who lost her home to the Eaton Fire is going viral for pragmatism and positivity in the face of so much adversity.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: Joan Plowright, Tony Award-winning British actor and widow of Laurence Olivier, dies at 95
item.description: Award-winning British actor Joan Plowright, who with her late husband Laurence Olivier did much to revitalize the U.K.'s theatrical scene in the decades after World War II, has died. She was 95.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Q&A: 'God Bless the USA' singer Lee Greenwood on Donald Trump's inauguration and Bibles
item.description: Forty years ago, Lee Greenwood released “God Bless the U.S.A.,” a patriotic anthem that has become a standard at political gatherings. He's performed the song for a number of presidents, including Ronald Reagan and George H. W. Bush.
item.dc:creator: THE ASSOCIATED PRESS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Top music acts announced for FireAid, a concert to support L.A. wildfire victims
item.description: The biggest names in pop, rock and country are coming together to raise funds for victims of the Palisades and Eaton wildfires and assist with the recovery.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about RedNote, a TikTok alternative
item.description: TikTok patrons are turning to a new platform in light of the nationwide ban set to shut down the app on Sunday. RedNote, a Chinese short-form video platform, is gaining ground as users look for an alternative to the social media company owned by the China's ByteDance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5
